channel.title: AI News — A Guide to Cloud & AI
channel.description: Daily AI news digest — curated headlines, summaries and trends

item.title: User found the perfect formula to make Excel misbehave
item.description: A user discovered a formula that causes Excel to misbehave, showcasing a fun side of tech support stories. This highlights the quirks and unexpected behaviors of software.

item.title: Qualcomm teases ‘dedicated CPU for agentic experiences’ and ‘agentic smartphones’
item.description: Qualcomm has announced plans for a dedicated CPU aimed at enhancing 'agentic experiences' in smartphones, marking its entry into custom AI silicon. This move could reshape mobile computing.

item.title: Fujitsu confirms mainframe biz to die in 2035, in time for quantum AI supercomputers to take over
item.description: Fujitsu plans to phase out its mainframe business by 2035, anticipating a shift towards quantum AI supercomputers and engaging in defense tech discussions with several countries. This transition reflects the evolving tech landscape.

item.title: ICANN opens applications for new generic top-level domains for the first time since 2012
item.description: ICANN has opened applications for new generic top-level domains for the first time since 2012, allowing businesses and individuals to propose new domain names. This could lead to a broader internet landscape.

item.title: Your TV can sound a lot better: 7 easy but unexpected ways to improve audio quality
item.description: This article provides seven practical tips to enhance TV audio quality without significant costs, making it easier for viewers to enjoy better sound. It addresses common audio issues faced by users.

item.title: I tested ChatGPT and Perplexity AI as my CarPlay voice assistants - this model was better
item.description: A comparison of ChatGPT and Perplexity AI as voice assistants in CarPlay reveals that both outperform Siri, with one model proving superior. This highlights advancements in AI-driven assistance.

item.title: ChatGPT Images 2.0 is a hit in India, but not a big winner elsewhere, yet
item.description: ChatGPT Images 2.0 has gained popularity in India for creating personal visuals, though its reception elsewhere has been lukewarm. This reflects regional differences in technology adoption.

item.title: How Shivon Zilis Operated as Elon Musk’s OpenAI Insider
item.description: Messages from a trial reveal Shivon Zilis acted as an intermediary between Elon Musk and OpenAI, providing insights into their relationship and the inner workings of the tech giant.

item.title: My dad needed a new desktop PC - here's why this Dell was the smartest choice
item.description: The article reviews a new Dell 24-inch All-in-One desktop PC, highlighting its compact design and solid performance, though it notes some limitations. This makes it a practical choice for users.

item.title: Phone users know when to hold ’em, delay upgrades amid inflation
item.description: As inflation rises, smartphone users are delaying upgrades, with average replacement cycles extending to 4.2 years. This trend indicates changing consumer behavior in tech spending.

item.title: Exclusive eBook: Inside the stealthy startup that pitched brainless human clones
item.description: R3 Bio, a stealth startup, proposes the controversial idea of creating brainless clones as backup human bodies, raising ethical concerns about immortality. This eBook delves into their vision and implications for humanity.

item.title: Bandwidth hogs rejoice, Celestica's latest switch is bristling with 64 ports of 1.6 Tbps Ethernet
item.description: Celestica has launched a new networking switch featuring 64 ports of 1.6 Tbps Ethernet, enhancing data transfer speeds significantly. This innovation supports Nvidia's latest NICs, catering to high-demand networking environments.

item.title: Enforcing trust and transparency: Open-sourcing the Azure Integrated HSM
item.description: Microsoft is open-sourcing its Azure Integrated HSM to enhance trust and transparency in cloud services, particularly for AI systems handling sensitive data. This move aims to ensure cryptographic integrity from hardware to services.

item.title: Microsoft boss tells investors the company is working to 'win back fans'
item.description: During an earnings call, Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella acknowledged the need to regain customer trust and loyalty, indicating a shift in strategy to address past missteps. This reflects the company's awareness of its brand perception.

item.title: Meta is running get-rich-quick ads for its AI tools
item.description: Meta's Manus AI is promoting ads that promise quick profits by helping local businesses establish online presence through AI tools. This campaign is part of a broader strategy to monetize AI capabilities.

item.title: The Download: the North Pole’s future and humanoid data
item.description: The Download newsletter discusses technological advancements related to climate change and historical research at the North Pole, emphasizing the importance of understanding our planet's past.

item.title: DeepSeek begins limited testing of vision mode
item.description: DeepSeek has begun limited testing of a new vision mode, expanding its capabilities beyond text to include multimodal features. This update enhances user experience by integrating visual data processing.

item.title: Gemini now lets users generate and download files directly in-app
item.description: Google's Gemini app now allows users to generate and download files directly within the app, streamlining the user experience and enhancing productivity. This feature aims to simplify workflows for users.

item.title: superharness 1.44.8
item.description: The latest update for Claude Code introduces a multi-agent session handoff framework, enhancing its functionality for developers. This update aims to improve collaboration and efficiency in coding environments.

item.title: Best 5 AI Video Generators Compared : See Which Delivers True Realism
item.description: A comparison of five AI video generators reveals their capabilities in producing realistic and imaginative video content. This analysis helps creators choose the right tool for their needs.

item.title: llmgate 0.8.0
item.description: The release of llmgate 0.8.0 introduces a lightweight library for calling various large language models from multiple providers, enhancing accessibility for developers.

item.title: langchain-agentskills 0.4.0
item.description: The langchain-agentskills 0.4.0 update adds support for LangChain and LangGraph, improving functionality for users in the AI development community.

item.title: Google Fixes CVSS 10 Gemini CLI CI RCE and Cursor Flaws Enable Code Execution
item.description: Google has fixed a critical security vulnerability in its Gemini CLI that could have allowed remote code execution, ensuring better security for users.

item.title: broccoli-ml 24.0.0
item.description: The broccoli-ml 24.0.0 release features useful PyTorch models expected to be relevant in 2025, showcasing advancements in machine learning.

item.title: pyg-nightly 2.8.0.dev20260430
item.description: The pyg-nightly 2.8.0.dev20260430 update introduces a Graph Neural Network Library for PyTorch, expanding options for researchers and developers.

item.title: iPhone Ultra Leak: Apple’s First Foldable Could Be Thinner Than the Galaxy Z Fold
item.description: Apple is set to launch its first foldable iPhone, the iPhone Ultra, which may redefine the smartphone market by combining phone and tablet features.

item.title: MARA accelerates mining-to-AI pivot with $1.5B Ohio power plant deal
item.description: MARA's acquisition of Long Ridge Energy for $1.5 billion marks a strategic move to pivot from Bitcoin mining to AI and high-performance computing infrastructure.

item.title: Captain, leader, far-right sympathiser: John Terry joins the ranks of football’s radicalised
item.description: John Terry's shift towards far-right sympathies reflects a broader trend of athletes being drawn into radical ideologies through online platforms.

item.title: Microsoft and OpenAI Amend Partnership to End Azure Exclusivity While Keeping Microsoft as Primary Cloud Partner
item.description: Microsoft and OpenAI have restructured their partnership, ending Azure exclusivity while maintaining Microsoft as the primary cloud partner, indicating a shift in strategy.

item.title: ocultar-mistral-mcp added to PyPI
item.description: The ocultar-mistral-mcp has been added to PyPI, providing zero-egress PII protection for Mistral Le Chat workflows, enhancing data privacy.

item.title: ocultar-mistral-mcp 0.1.0
item.description: The release of Mistral MCP 0.1.0 introduces zero-egress PII protection for workflows, enhancing data privacy in AI applications.

item.title: age-kit added to PyPI
item.description: The AI Game Developer Kit has been added to PyPI, allowing developers to create complete games using pre-built components.

item.title: Elon Musk vs Sam Altman Lawsuit Update: Elon Musk Testifies Against Sam Altman in Explosive OpenAI Trial
item.description: Elon Musk testified against Sam Altman in a lawsuit, accusing OpenAI's leaders of straying from their mission to benefit humanity.

item.title: ai-rulez 4.1.1
item.description: AI-rulez 4.1.1 offers a complete development workflow for over 19 tools, simplifying configuration generation for various AI models.

item.title: AI-generated UI is inaccessible by default
item.description: AI-generated user interfaces are often inaccessible by default, emphasizing the need for developers to ensure accessibility in their code.

item.title: kanon-kit 0.3.0a7
item.description: Kanon-kit 0.3.0a7 is a portable development kit for LLM-agent-driven repositories, compatible with various AI tools.

item.title: elitea-sdk 0.6.19
item.description: Elitea-sdk 0.6.19 provides a software development kit for building langchain agents, expanding resources for developers.

item.title: 【AX INSIGHT｜AI実践伴走レポート 2026年4月号】株式会社AX、「伴走現場で見えた、企業のAI活用の次の一歩」を公開
item.description: AX Corporation released a report on AI utilization in businesses, outlining the next steps for effective AI implementation.

item.title: meshagent-anthropic 0.39.4
item.description: Meshagent 0.39.4 introduces Anthropic Building Blocks, enhancing the capabilities of the Meshagent framework.

item.title: GeminiがPDF・Word・Excelなどを出力可能に
item.description: Google's Gemini AI can now generate files in various formats, including PDF and Word, directly from chat interactions.

item.title: langgraph-postgres-memory added to PyPI
item.description: LangGraph has added a production-ready PostgreSQL memory class to PyPI, facilitating easier management of memory for LangGraph agents. This integration enhances the efficiency of operations within the LangGraph framework.

item.title: Samsung SmartThings now supports IKEA Matter devices, IKEA smart home hub no longer required
item.description: Samsung's SmartThings platform now supports IKEA's Matter-enabled devices, eliminating the need for a separate IKEA smart home hub. This integration simplifies smart home management for users.

item.title: Xiaomi Just Released an Open-Source AI That Outperforms GPT-5.4
item.description: Xiaomi has launched MiMo V2.5 Pro, an open-source AI model that reportedly outperforms GPT-5.4. It features a unique architecture that optimizes efficiency by activating a subset of its parameters for each task.

item.title: langchain-seahorse 0.4.1
item.description: The latest update of LangChain introduces version 0.4.1, which integrates the VectorStore with the Seahorse API Gateway. This enhancement aims to improve data handling in AI applications.

item.title: Gemini just made it much easier to turn ideas into real files
item.description: Gemini has improved its AI chatbot functionality, making it easier to convert AI-generated ideas into usable files without extensive copy-pasting. This advancement enhances user experience in document creation.

item.title: They Are Dumbing Down AI on Purpose â Hereâs Why Itâs a Globalist Power Grab
item.description: An article discusses concerns about intentional limitations being placed on AI capabilities, suggesting a broader agenda to control AI development. It argues that this 'AI lobotomy' undermines the potential of intelligent systems.

item.title: Apple A20 Chip Likely To Miss Out On New WMCM Packaging Tech That Allows For Various CPU/GPU Core Combos
item.description: Apple's upcoming A20 chip may not utilize new WMCM packaging technology due to ongoing DRAM shortages, indicating potential limitations in performance for the iPhone 18. This reflects broader supply chain challenges affecting tech companies.

item.title: torchaid 0.1.2
item.description: The torchaid library version 0.1.2 has been released, designed to simplify the deep learning training pipeline in PyTorch. This utility aims to enhance the efficiency of model training.

item.title: Muhammad Aqeel: Volatile Queries and Semantic Caching: How to Make Sure It Always Returns the Right Answer
item.description: Part 3 of the Semantic Caching in PostgreSQL series discusses volatile queries and caching strategies to ensure accurate results. The series aims to improve the efficiency of database interactions with AI models.

item.title: ollama-client added to PyPI
item.description: The ollama-client has been added to PyPI, providing a lightweight command-line interface for connecting to a remote Ollama server. This tool aims to streamline interactions with the Ollama platform.
